name: azure-keyvault-keys-rust
description: Azure Key Vault Keys SDK for Rust. Use for creating, managing, and using cryptographic keys in Azure Key Vault with the Rust programming language.
capabilities: Create and manage RSA and elliptic curve cryptographic keys in Azure Key Vault
Perform encrypt, decrypt, sign, and verify operations using keys stored in Key Vault
Backup and restore keys for disaster recovery scenarios
List, retrieve, and delete keys with proper lifecycle management
Use hardware security module (HSM) protected keys for sensitive workloads
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only skill containing only documentation (SKILL.md) with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files and detected 0 security issues. The skill provides safe guidance for using the Azure Key Vault Keys SDK in Rust for cryptographic key management.
valueStatement: Need to securely manage cryptographic keys in Azure Key Vault from Rust applications. This skill provides comprehensive guidance for using the Azure Key Vault Keys SDK to create, manage, and perform cryptographic operations with keys.
userTitle: Manage Azure Key Vault Keys with Rust
limitations: Does not provide actual key material access - keys stay protected in Azure
Requires valid Azure credentials and network access to Key Vault
Does not manage key rotation policies automatically

useCases.targetUser: Cloud developers building secure Rust applications
useCases.title: Secure Key Management for Cloud Applications
useCases.description: Store and manage cryptographic keys securely in Azure Key Vault instead of in application code

useCases.title: HSM-Protected Key Operations
useCases.description: Use hardware security module protected keys for compliance and high-security requirements

useCases.title: Cryptographic Operations Without Key Exposure
useCases.description: Perform signing and encryption operations without exposing private keys to applications

promptTemplates.title: Basic Key Creation
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I create a new RSA key in Azure Key Vault using the Rust SDK?
promptTemplates.scenario: Developer needs to create a new cryptographic key

promptTemplates.title: EC Key for Signing
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create an elliptic curve key for digital signatures with the Azure Key Vault Rust SDK.
promptTemplates.scenario: Developer needs EC key for signing operations

promptTemplates.title: Key Backup and Restore
promptTemplates.prompt: What is the process to backup and restore a key in Azure Key Vault using Rust?

promptTemplates.title: Production Best Practices
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the best practices for using Azure Key Vault keys in production Rust applications?

bestPractices: Use ManagedIdentityCredential in production instead of hardcoded credentials
Enable soft delete on Key Vaults to recover accidentally deleted keys
Use key rotation by creating new key versions periodically
antiPatterns: Storing key material in code or environment variables instead of Key Vault
Using developer credentials in production environments
Disabling soft delete on production Key Vaults

faq.question: What key types does Azure Key Vault support?
faq.answer: Azure Key Vault supports RSA keys (2048, 3072, 4096 bits), elliptic curve keys (P-256, P-384, P-521), and their HSM-protected variants.

faq.question: How does authentication work with this SDK?
faq.answer: The SDK uses azure_identity for authentication. Use DeveloperToolsCredential for local development and ManagedIdentityCredential when deployed to Azure.

faq.question: Can I perform cryptographic operations without downloading keys?
faq.answer: Yes, Key Vault can perform encrypt, decrypt, sign, verify, wrap, and unwrap operations remotely without exposing private key material.

faq.question: What is the difference between soft delete and purge?
faq.answer: Soft delete allows recovery of deleted keys within a retention period. Purge permanently deletes the key and cannot be undone.

faq.question: How do I backup and restore keys?
faq.answer: Use the backup_key method to get encrypted backup bytes, store them securely. Use restore_key with the backup to recover the key.

faq.question: What RBAC roles are needed for key operations?
faq.answer: Key Vault Crypto User role allows using keys for operations. Key Vault Crypto Officer role provides full CRUD permissions on keys.
